release_index_element_identifiers.summary: Canonical release-facing identity anchors such as release id, release series, deterministic fingerprints, and suite or product references where relevant.
Reference or embedded fields aligned with the upstream release contract profile.
Classification of release candidates into explicit channels or lanes for policy interpretation.
Broad target-family references plus exact-target compatibility descriptors.
Build ids, manifest hashes, content hashes, component graph hashes, install-profile ids, and related provenance anchors.
Deprecation, yank, supersession, and availability posture relevant to lawful selection.

resolution_input_classes.notes: Resolution must use the explicit compatibility envelope rather than version-string equality.
Semantic contract mismatch is not repairable by channel or version shorthand.
Wire, schema, and format compatibility remain layered and explicit.
Exact target truth remains machine-facing and finer-grained than family filters.
Family labels may narrow the search space but may not decide exact compatibility alone.
Channels influence policy after compatibility truth is preserved.
Lifecycle state may affect admissibility or ranking under explicit policy.
Governance and trust posture remain explicit and must not be guessed from channel names.
Install-profile preferences may shape candidate sets when explicitly governed.
These anchors support reconstruction and deterministic selection but do not create compatibility.

identity_vs_compatibility_vs_selection_d.identity: Declares which governed release record or artifact set the candidate is.
identity_vs_compatibility_vs_selection_d.compatibility: Declares whether the candidate lawfully fits the request, environment, and governed compatibility envelope.
identity_vs_compatibility_vs_selection_d.selection: Declares which compatible admissible candidate should be chosen under explicit policy.
rules: A candidate may have valid identity and still be incompatible.
Several candidates may be compatible and still require policy-based selection.
Selection must not reinterpret build identity, channel, or filenames as substitutes for compatibility truth.

channel_lane_policy_rules: Channel and lane labels are policy classifiers, not compatibility envelopes.
Channel or lane preference may shape the candidate set only after explicit compatibility checks remain in force.
Channel shorthand must not override release contract profile mismatches.
Local aliases or policy-only labels do not create new compatibility semantics by themselves.
target_family_vs_exact_target_distinctio: Target family is a broad grouping aid for matrix navigation and coarse filtering.
Exact target descriptors carry concrete OS, ABI, arch, platform, and environment meaning.
Resolution may use both, but exact-target admissibility must not be approximated casually by family labels alone.

canonical_vs_derived_distinctions.rule: Derived or projection surfaces may expose selection context but may not redefine release identity, compatibility, or selection law.

ambiguities_eliminated: latest version alone does not resolve compatibility
channels do not replace release-contract-profile checks
filenames do not define resolution truth
target family alone does not give exact target compatibility
